name: resources
context: Expose contextual data sources via MCP resources — files, database schemas, API responses identified by URIs
info: MCP resources are application-controlled data sources that servers expose to provide context to AI applications. Unlike tools (which are model-invoked), resources are selected by the application or user to enrich the LLM's context. Each resource is identified by a URI (file://, https://, custom schemes), has a name, optional description, and MIME type. Resources support text and binary content, URI templates for parameterized access, subscriptions for real-time updates, and list change notifications. Servers declare the `resources` capability during initialization, optionally with `subscribe: true` and `listChanged: true`. Resources are discovered via `resources/list`, read via `resources/read`, and templates listed via `resources/templates/list`.
triggers: add a resource to my MCP server
expose a database schema as a resource

sections.title: URI schemes and resource identification
sections.body: Resources are identified by URIs following RFC 3986. Standard schemes: `file://` for filesystem-like resources (don't need to map to actual files), `https://` for web resources the client can fetch directly, `git://` for version control. Custom schemes like `db://`, `config://`, `schema://` are allowed and should be documented. URI templates (RFC 6570) enable parameterized access patterns like `file:///{path}` or `db:///{table}/{id}`.

sections.title: Resource subscriptions
sections.body: Servers can support resource subscriptions by declaring `subscribe: true` in the resources capability. Clients subscribe via `resources/subscribe` with a resource URI. When the resource changes, the server sends `notifications/resources/updated`. The client then re-reads the resource via `resources/read` to get updated content. This enables real-time context updates — e.g., watching a config file for changes.

sections.title: Text and binary content
sections.body: Resource contents can be text (returned in `text` field) or binary (returned as base64-encoded data in `blob` field). The `mimeType` indicates the content type. Common MIME types: `text/plain`, `text/markdown`, `application/json`, `text/x-python`, `image/png`. For directories, use `inode/directory` XDG MIME type.

links.context: Parent skill for building MCP servers
Tools can return resource links and embedded resources

related.note: Resources are one of three primitives servers expose

depends.reason: Resources are a primitive of MCP servers
